name: Community Food Hub @ Malvern Family Resource Centre
address: Outside of Malvern Family Resource Centre, 90 Littles Rd, Scarborough, ON M1B 5E2
description: Community Food Hub, a volunteer-led organization, is on a mission to decrease food insecurity and avoidable food waste by providing low-barrier outdoor community fridges/pantries in Scarborough.
eligibility: Anyone can access the community fridge/pantry, no questions asked. Take what you need, leave what you can.

name: The Children’s Book Bank
address: 1154 Morningside Ave, Scarborough ON, M1B 3A4
description: Operates drop-in community literacy spaces that are open to all children, teens, and their families. These spaces offer free literacy programs and a wide selection of free books for visitors to choose from. We also host free visits to our spaces for schools, daycares and summer camps.
languages_spoken: Primarily English, but we often have books in other languages!
eligibility: Children, teens, and their families
additional_info: We are located on the second floor of the Pleasant Corner Mall. Walk around to the back of the building (follow signs for Kumon) and go up the stairs or ramp. Enter through the first door on your left. You’ll see the Book Bank at the end of the hall, next to the dentist's office.
